verses.text: Job opens his final defence: 'I made a covenant with mine eyes' not to look lustfully on a woman, knowing God sees all his ways. He has guarded even his glances. Real purity begins in the eyes and the heart, under the gaze of the God who sees in secret.

If he ever walked in falsehood or hurried after deceit, let God weigh him in honest scales. He invites the strictest test. A clear conscience does not fear examination but welcomes the balance of truth.

If he ever walked in falsehood or hurried after deceit, let God weigh him in honest scales. He invites the strictest test. A clear conscience does not fear examination but welcomes the balance of truth.

If his heart was ever enticed to his neighbour's wife, he owns it would be a heinous, judgment-worthy crime. He treats adultery as a fire that consumes all. The godly man hates the sin others excuse, knowing its ruin.

He never despised the cause of his servants, for 'did not he that made me... make him?' One Maker formed them both. To wrong the lowly is to forget that master and servant stand equal before their Creator.

He never despised the cause of his servants, for 'did not he that made me... make him?' One Maker formed them both. To wrong the lowly is to forget that master and servant stand equal before their Creator.

He never despised the cause of his servants, for 'did not he that made me... make him?' One Maker formed them both. To wrong the lowly is to forget that master and servant stand equal before their Creator.

He never turned away the poor, the widow, or the fatherless, but cared for them as a father. His religion reached the needy. True godliness shows itself in tender provision for those who cannot repay.

He never turned away the poor, the widow, or the fatherless, but cared for them as a father. His religion reached the needy. True godliness shows itself in tender provision for those who cannot repay.

He never turned away the poor, the widow, or the fatherless, but cared for them as a father. His religion reached the needy. True godliness shows itself in tender provision for those who cannot repay.

He never let any perish for lack of clothing, for 'destruction from God was a terror to me.' The fear of God restrained his hand from cruelty. Reverence for the Almighty is the surest guard of justice and mercy toward men.

He never made gold his hope nor secretly worshipped the sun or moon, for that would be to deny the God above. His heart was not divided. To trust riches or creatures is a hidden idolatry that betrays the living God.

He never gloated at an enemy's downfall, and his door was always open to the stranger. His kindness reached even foes and outsiders. Love that blesses enemies and welcomes the wayfarer bears the mark of God.

He never hid his sin 'as Adam,' covering it for fear of the crowd. He dealt openly with his faults. The honest heart confesses rather than conceals, refusing to wear a mask before God or men.
He never hid his sin 'as Adam,' covering it for fear of the crowd. He dealt openly with his faults. The honest heart confesses rather than conceals, refusing to wear a mask before God or men.

Job longs that God would hear him and that his accuser would write out the charge, which he would wear like a crown and answer step by step. He is sure of his innocence. The upright can face any honest indictment without dread.

If his land was gained by fraud or its workers wronged, let it bear thorns instead of wheat. With this final oath, 'the words of Job are ended.' He rests his case wholly before God. Having spoken all, the sufferer falls silent and waits.
